title: Minimal Git CI using hooks
content_html:
I have a personal server where I am hosting my git repos. Setting up a new git repo is easy: I just run ssh server git init --bare repo.[1] That's it. Cloning my newly created repo is as simple as git clone server:repo.

For some projects I've needed a CI, and I looked around for some existing solutions. The CI systems I found are convoluted, yaml-ridden, slow, messy or hard to self-host.

Luckily, I do not need many "modern" CI features like complete isolation for my builds or secret management. I just need something that runs some tests, builds a project or moves some files.

What I have settled on is a simple CI using git hooks. Specifically, I added a remote post-receive hook, which is a hook that runs whenever I push to a project. It is just a shell-script that I add to the hooks directory in a bare git repository.

The thing to watch out for with post-receive hooks is if the script fails, the code you push will be rejected. Also, if the script is slow, pushing will take a while. You generally don't want either. I solved this by using nq, a minimal job queue, to queue up my jobs in the background instead. The result is quite sleek: my post-receive hook just executes nq, and I can access the logs by running ssh server nqtail -a.

The CI runs instantly, and is very easy to set up, so I am happy with the result. If you'd like to set it up for yourself, I created a short tutorial.

There are many ways you could expand the base I provide in the tutorial: you could sandbox the builds (for example with landdown), use podman to isolate builds from the environment, or use sops to manage secrets. For bazaar-style development, receiving git patches via email should be enough. You can also set up cathedral-style development using git-shell or git http-backend.

[1] Thanks to Michał Bartoszkiewicz for showing me an even simpler way to create a new repository. Reply

sentences.en: There are many ways you could expand the base I provide in the tutorial: you could sandbox the builds (for example with landdown), use podman to isolate builds from the environment, or use sops to manage secrets.
sentences.zh: 有很多方法可以扩展我在教程中提供的基础：您可以对构建进行沙箱处理（例如使用 landdown），使用 podman 将构建与环境隔离，或者使用 sops 来管理机密。

sentences.en: For bazaar-style development, receiving git patches via email should be enough.
sentences.zh: 对于集市式开发，通过电子邮件接收 git 补丁就足够了。

sentences.en: You can also set up cathedral-style development using git-shell or git http-backend.
sentences.zh: 您还可以使用 git-shell 或 git http-backend 设置大教堂式开发。

content_html_zh:
我有一个个人服务器，用于托管我的 git 存储库。 设置新的 git 存储库很简单：我只需运行 ssh server git init --bare repo。[1]就是这样。 克隆我新创建的存储库就像 git clone server:repo 一样简单。

对于某些项目，我需要 CI，并且我四处寻找一些现有的解决方案。 我发现的 CI 系统错综复杂、充斥着 yaml、缓慢、混乱或难以自行托管。

幸运的是，我不需要许多“现代”CI 功能，例如完全隔离我的构建或秘密管理。 我只需要运行一些测试、构建项目或移动一些文件的东西。

我选择的是使用 git hooks 的简单 CI。 具体来说，我添加了一个远程接收后挂钩，该挂钩在我推送到项目时运行。 它只是一个 shell 脚本，我将其添加到裸 git 存储库中的 hooks 目录中。

使用 post-receive hooks 需要注意的是，如果脚本失败，您推送的代码将被拒绝。 另外，如果脚本很慢，推送将需要一段时间。 通常你也不想要。 我通过使用 nq（一个最小作业队列）在后台对我的作业进行排队来解决这个问题。 结果非常流畅：我的 post-receive 挂钩只执行 nq，我可以通过运行 ssh server nqtail -a 来访问日志。

CI 可以立即运行，并且非常容易设置，所以我对结果很满意。 如果您想自己设置，我创建了一个简短的教程。

有很多方法可以扩展我在教程中提供的基础：您可以对构建进行沙箱处理（例如使用 landdown），使用 podman 将构建与环境隔离，或者使用 sops 来管理机密。 对于集市式开发，通过电子邮件接收 git 补丁就足够了。 您还可以使用 git-shell 或 git http-backend 设置大教堂式开发。

[1] Thanks to Michał Bartoszkiewicz for showing me an even simpler way to create a new repository. Reply
